tiTabelionatos.headline1: Your notary office could shut down.
tiTabelionatos.description: CNJ Provision 213 is not a recommendation — it's a binding regulation with defined deadlines, mandatory stages and personal liability for the holder. If your notary office lacks tested backups, an implemented firewall, audit trails, a continuity plan and data encryption, you're already non-compliant. And when the regulatory authority inspects, it won't matter if the problem was technical. The responsibility always falls on the notary. Operational downtime isn't just lost revenue. It's compromised legal acts, exposed sensitive client data, reputational risk and, in severe cases, disciplinary sanctions. Provision 213 requires RPO, RTO, BCP, DRP, immutable audit trails, vulnerability management, network segmentation, multi-factor authentication and a technical dossier — all on deadlines that are already running. WMTi specializes in IT infrastructure for notary offices. We don't sell generic technology. We deliver documented compliance, operational continuity and the peace of mind that when inspection comes, everything will be in order.

faq.question: Does Provision 213 apply to notary offices?
faq.answer: Yes. CNJ Provision 213 applies to all notarial and registry services in Brazil, including notary offices, regardless of class or volume of acts.

faq.question: What are the deadlines for Provision 213 compliance?
faq.answer: Stages 1 and 2 must be completed in 90 days (Class 3), 150 days (Class 2) or 210 days (Class 1). Full implementation of all 5 stages must be finalized within 24, 30 or 36 months, depending on class.

faq.question: What happens if my notary office doesn't comply?
faq.answer: Unjustified non-compliance may lead to administrative disciplinary proceedings, plus civil and criminal liabilities. Responsibility is personal and non-delegable.

faq.question: My backup is set up. Should I worry?
faq.answer: If it's never been formally tested, yes. Provision 213 requires documented restoration tests — biannual for Class 3 and annual for Classes 1 and 2. Untested backup is a serious risk.

faq.question: Does WMTi prepare the technical dossier required by the regulatory authority?
faq.answer: Yes. We deliver a technical dossier with documentary evidence, integrity hashes, test records and compliance reports for each implemented stage.

faq.question: Do I need multi-factor authentication in my notary office?
faq.answer: Yes. Provision 213 requires mandatory MFA for administrative access, system management, databases and critical functions. Shared credentials are expressly prohibited.

faq.question: Does WMTi serve notary offices outside the Paraíba Valley?
faq.answer: Yes. We serve notary offices across Brazil, with remote and on-site support as needed.

faq.question: What are RPO and RTO and why does my notary office need to define them?
faq.answer: RPO is the maximum acceptable data loss point and RTO is the maximum time to restore operations. Provision 213 defines limits by class: Class 3 requires 4h RPO and 8h RTO. Without these in your BCP/DRP, the office is non-compliant.

provimento213.changesDescription: Provision 213/2026 goes far beyond the former Provision 74. Formal security policies, business continuity plans (BCP/DRP), encryption, immutable audit trails, vulnerability management, and penetration testing are now mandatory. Responsibility remains personal to the registry holder, even with outsourced IT.

changesCards.title: Personal liability
changesCards.text: The registry holder remains personally liable for compliance, even when using outsourced systems or cloud services.

changesCards.title: Defined deadlines
changesCards.text: Initial stages range from 90 to 210 days, with full implementation within 24–36 months depending on class.

changesCards.title: Mandatory encryption
changesCards.text: AES-256 or stronger for data in transit, at rest, and in backups. Obsolete algorithms are forbidden.

changesCards.title: Immutable audit trails
changesCards.text: Logs must be protected against tampering, retained for at least five years, and synchronized via NTP.

changesCards.title: Defined RPO and RTO
changesCards.text: Mandatory maximum data loss (RPO) and recovery time (RTO) parameters are set by class.

changesCards.title: Built-in LGPD compliance
changesCards.text: Compliance with Brazil’s data protection law is explicit: DPO, records of processing, and incident management are required.

faq.question: Do clinics need their own server?
faq.answer: It depends on the size. For clinics with more than 5 workstations and systems like EMR and PACS, a dedicated Dell PowerEdge server ensures performance, security, and centralized backup. For smaller offices, cloud solutions may suffice.

faq.question: How to protect electronic medical records?
faq.answer: We implement automated backup with AES-256 encryption, role-based access control, IDS/IPS firewall, managed antivirus, and audit logs — all in compliance with LGPD for sensitive health data.

faq.question: Are clinics targeted by ransomware?
faq.answer: Yes. Healthcare institutions are priority ransomware targets because they hold sensitive data and have high urgency to restore systems. We implement layered defense: pfSense firewall, network segmentation, isolated backup, and incident response plan.

faq.question: How to ensure secure backup of exams and medical data?
faq.answer: We use the 3-2-1 strategy: three copies of data, on two different media types, with one offsite copy (Azure cloud). Backup is automated, encrypted, and tested monthly to ensure fast recovery.

faq.question: Why is internet redundancy important in clinics?
faq.answer: Modern medical systems depend on constant connectivity for EMR, telemedicine, and lab integration. We implement automatic failover with two or more connections to ensure continuous availability.
